artists.name: Riley Kingston
artists.bio: Riley Kingston is a modern electric blues guitarist and vocalist known for gritty Chicago-style riffs and soulful songwriting. After years on the club circuit, he founded South Side Ember Records to highlight contemporary blues voices.

artists.name: Maria Lopez Band
artists.bio: Fronted by powerhouse vocalist Maria Lopez, the band blends West Coast jump blues with gospel-tinged harmonies. Their live sets are driven by horn stabs, call-and-response choruses, and extended vocal improvisations.

artists.name: The Delta Runners
artists.bio: The Delta Runners are a quartet that channels raw Mississippi delta blues with contemporary arrangements, featuring slide guitar, harmonica, and minimalist percussion.

artists.name: Chicago Fire Blues Band
artists.bio: Chicago Fire Blues Band is a hard-hitting electric blues outfit rooted firmly in the tradition of the Windy City, with searing guitar leads and a tight rhythm section.

artists.name: Lena Hart
artists.bio: Lena Hart is a singer-songwriter whose smoky contralto voice and piano-led arrangements draw from classic torch songs, urban blues, and soul ballads.

artists.name: The Midnight Ramblers
artists.bio: The Midnight Ramblers are a five-piece band specializing in moody, late-night blues with touches of psychedelia and roots rock, often recorded live to tape.

artists.name: Hazel James Trio
artists.bio: Led by guitarist and vocalist Hazel James, this trio strips blues back to its essentials with raw, intimate performances and extended improvisations.

artists.name: Smokehouse Johnson
artists.bio: Smokehouse Johnson is a veteran harmonica player and storyteller who mixes front-porch acoustic blues with electric band arrangements.

artists.name: Northside Electric
artists.bio: Northside Electric is a Chicago-based collective that fuses blues grooves with modern production, mixing analog tape warmth and subtle electronic textures.

artists.name: Bones & Brass Band
artists.bio: Bones & Brass Band blends New Orleans second-line rhythms with brass-heavy blues arrangements, delivering high-energy festival sets.

blog_posts.title: Analog Tape in a Digital World: How We Track Modern Blues Records
blog_posts.content: For many of our artists, the sound of blues belongs on analog tape. In this studio deep dive, engineer Carla Nguyen walks through our hybrid workflow: tracking rhythm sections to 2-inch tape, printing vocal comps through outboard compressors, and finally capturing high-resolution digital transfers for mixing and editing. We cover why analog recording still matters for feel and saturation, how we align tape machines before a session, and what to listen for when choosing tape formulations for electric versus acoustic blues projects.
blog_posts.excerpt: Engineer Carla Nguyen explains our hybrid analog tape and digital workflow for modern blues sessions.

blog_posts.title: Choosing Your First Analog Recording Rig for Blues Sessions
blog_posts.content: If you are a blues artist looking to add analog character to your recordings, you do not need to buy a full 24-track console and two-inch tape deck on day one. In this guide we break down realistic starter setups: from cassette four-tracks that pair well with lo-fi country blues to half-inch 8-track machines ideal for live-in-the-room band recordings. We compare the sonic differences between reel-to-reel formats, explain the cost of tape stock, and show how to integrate analog recording paths with a basic USB audio interface and laptop.
blog_posts.excerpt: A practical guide to building a beginner-friendly analog recording setup for blues artists.

blog_posts.title: Tracking Live to Tape with a Full Band
blog_posts.content: For our recent Hazel James Trio sessions, we recorded the entire band live to a 16-track tape machine. This article breaks down the microphone choices, room layout, and mixing philosophy behind capturing live blues energy in a single take. We discuss why bleed can be a musical asset, how analog recording encourages more focused performances, and the minimal overdubs we used to preserve the feel.
blog_posts.excerpt: Inside our live-to-tape session with Hazel James Trio and how we embraced bleed and performance over perfection.

blog_posts.title: Inside the "Midnight Crossroads" Sessions with The Midnight Ramblers
blog_posts.content: The Midnight Ramblers spent five winter nights cutting "Midnight Crossroads" mostly live on the floor. In this story we recap how the band arranged the material, what guitars and amps made the final cuts, and why we kept long, unedited solos on several tracks. Fans of deep, late-night blues jams will appreciate the raw performances captured during these sessions.
blog_posts.excerpt: Go behind the scenes of The Midnight Ramblers' new album "Midnight Crossroads."

blog_posts.title: Summer 2025 Tour Dates Announced
blog_posts.content: We are excited to announce new summer 2025 tour dates for Chicago Fire Blues Band, Maria Lopez Band, and Smokehouse Johnson. Cities include Chicago, New York, Austin, and Memphis, with a mix of club shows and festival appearances. Many dates feature special guests from our roster.
blog_posts.excerpt: Chicago Fire Blues Band, Maria Lopez Band, and more hit the road in summer 2025.

blog_posts.title: Lena Hart Announces Live Album Recorded in Chicago
blog_posts.content: Lena Hart is returning with a new live album captured over two sold-out nights in Chicago. The record highlights her extended vocal improvisations, intimate piano moments, and a fiery backing band.
blog_posts.excerpt: Two nights in Chicago become Lena Hart's first live album.

blog_posts.title: Pressing Vinyl the Right Way: Our Approach to Blues LPs
blog_posts.content: From lacquer cutting to test pressings, we obsess over every stage of the vinyl manufacturing process. This article outlines how we master specifically for vinyl, why we avoid overly hot cuts that distort harmonica and cymbals, and how we evaluate test pressings in the studio before approving a run.
blog_posts.excerpt: A look at how we master and manufacture vinyl for our blues catalog.

blog_posts.title: Collecting Rare '60s Blues LPs on a Budget
blog_posts.content: You do not need deep pockets to start a serious '60s blues vinyl collection. We highlight some overlooked regional pressings and reissues that still sound incredible and can be found for under $30 at record fairs and online marketplaces.
blog_posts.excerpt: Affordable paths into rare 1960s blues on vinyl.

blog_posts.title: Maria Lopez on Carrying the Torch for Female Voices in Blues
blog_posts.content: Maria Lopez reflects on the legacy of women in blues, from classic pioneers to current innovators. She shares her approach to vocal phrasing, stage presence, and arranging horn lines that support a powerful lead voice.
blog_posts.excerpt: An interview with Maria Lopez about the past, present, and future of women in blues.

blog_posts.title: Northside Electric and the Art of Hybrid Blues Production
blog_posts.content: Northside Electric blends classic blues structures with subtle electronic textures. In this article, we explore how the band uses analog amps, live drums, and tube microphones alongside synth pads and programmed percussion without losing the feel of the blues.
blog_posts.excerpt: How Northside Electric walks the line between tradition and experimentation.

blog_posts.title: Smokehouse Johnson Takes the Blues Back to the Porch
blog_posts.content: For his latest project, Smokehouse Johnson recorded a series of field sessions on back porches and in living rooms across the Midwest. We talk about capturing the ambience of cicadas, train whistles, and distant traffic as part of the performances.
blog_posts.excerpt: Behind the scenes of Smokehouse Johnson's intimate field recordings.

blog_posts.title: How We Master Digital Blues Releases for Streaming
blog_posts.content: Mastering for streaming services is different from cutting a hot 7-inch single. We explain our target loudness levels, how we preserve dynamics for guitar solos and drum transients, and why we still run mixes through analog gear before final digital limiting.
blog_posts.excerpt: Our philosophy for mastering modern digital blues releases.

blog_posts.title: Analog vs Digital: Mythbusting for Blues Artists
blog_posts.content: There are plenty of myths about analog recording being automatically better than digital. In this article, we clarify which aspects of the sound come from tape saturation, transformers, and consoles, and which come down to performance, arrangement, and mic placement.
blog_posts.excerpt: We separate fact from fiction in the analog vs digital debate for blues musicians.

product_variants.title: Lena Hart: Live in Chicago (Digital Album)
Dusty Strings (1967 Original Sessions) Vinyl LP
Chicago Fire Blues Band: Live 2020 Vinyl LP

products.name: Porchlight Stories
products.description: An intimate collection of front-porch acoustic blues from Smokehouse Johnson, captured on location across the Midwest with creaking chairs, cicadas, and late-night trains in the background.

products.name: Blue Line Stories
products.description: Maria Lopez Band rides the late-night trains of Chicago in this horn-driven studio album, blending jump blues, gospel-tinged choruses, and powerful female vocals.

products.name: Neon City Nights
products.description: Northside Electric fuses analog amps and live drums with subtle electronic textures on this moody, late-night blues record colored by neon reflections and echoing side streets.

products.name: Midnight Crossroads (Standard Edition)
products.description: The core eight-track version of The Midnight Ramblers' deep, nocturnal blues journey, tracked mostly live to tape with extended guitar solos and organ swells.

products.name: Midnight Crossroads (Deluxe Edition)
products.description: The expanded deluxe edition of "Midnight Crossroads" featuring additional outtakes, alternate takes, and a long closing jam captured during the same live-to-tape sessions.

products.name: Lena Hart: Live in Chicago
products.description: Recorded over two sold-out nights, Lena Hart stretches out at the piano with soaring, smoky vocals and a dynamic band captured in front of an intimate Chicago audience.

products.name: Dusty Strings (1967 Original Sessions)
products.description: A remastered 1967 studio date from a young Smokehouse Johnson, featuring gritty electric band arrangements cut straight to tape. Faithfully restored and pressed on heavyweight vinyl.

products.name: Midnight Express (1974)
products.description: A hard-hitting 1974 set from Chicago Fire Blues Band, full of raw guitar tones and stomping shuffles, cut from the original analog master tapes.

products.name: City Lights (1983)
products.description: A slick yet soulful early-1980s outing from The Delta Runners, balancing glossy production with down-home slide guitar and street-corner stories.

products.name: Chicago Fire Blues Band: Live 2020
products.description: Captured across several fiery club dates, this live LP bottles the full-volume power of Chicago Fire Blues Band at the dawn of the new decade.

products.name: Classic Logo T-Shirt - Blue
products.description: Soft cotton unisex T-shirt in deep blue featuring the South Side Ember Records logo on the front.

products.name: 2025 Tour T-Shirt - Blue
products.description: Official 2025 label tour T-shirt in royal blue with dates listed on the back.

products.name: South Side Ember Records Poster
products.description: Full-color 18" x 24" poster featuring the South Side Ember Records logo and a collage of classic blues imagery.

products.name: Blues Vinyl Tote Bag
products.description: Sturdy black canvas tote bag sized to carry up to a dozen LPs, printed with a stylized turntable and South Side Ember branding.
